title: Llama7B attention measured compute energy closure
hypothesis: The command-calibrated HBM frontier still assumes an abstract 524288 MAC/cycle compute point on a 400 mm2 die. Replacing that capacity with measured dense-tile replicated compute rows should expose whether the current best family remains physically plausible and should produce a corrected throughput/energy/area frontier.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does measured dense-tile compute capacity preserve or replace the current HBM-calibrated Llama7B frontier?
include: consume the merged HBM command-calibrated service result
consume the merged dense-tile measured compute substitution result
check whether the abstract selected MAC/cycle target fits the selected die using measured tile density
recompute latency, token throughput, compute energy, HBM energy, SRAM energy, and NoC energy for measured compute rows
report measured-compute-constrained latency-best, energy-best, Pareto rows, and remaining abstractions
follow_on_broad_sweep: If measured compute replaces the abstract frontier, use the corrected point for the next architecture decision and explore denser compute or lower precision.
If measured compute preserves the family, promote the current HBM-calibrated frontier and move to NoC/SRAM service closure.

expected_benefit: Prevents a physically infeasible MAC/cycle assumption from driving the Llama7B frontier.
Makes compute power/energy use measured dense-tile PPA rather than nearest-reference scaling only.
Reports both measured compute capacity and source-backed HBM energy in one comparable frontier.
risks: Compute power is still replicated from measured tiles, not measured as one full integrated macro.
Compute energy ranking assumes clock gating during added HBM command-service stalls; wall-time energy is reported as an upper bound.
HBM energy remains source-backed aggregate pJ/byte rather than vendor stack-current signoff.

required_evaluations.objective: Re-rank the Llama7B HBM-calibrated frontier under measured dense-tile compute capacity and measured-tile compute power.

expected_result.reason: Determine whether the abstract 524288 MAC/cycle selected point is physically plausible under measured dense-tile compute density and energy.
